prompt: Build a Local Insights Review & Experimentation Portal that helps a product team manage user-generated feedback and analyze behavior entirely within an offline network. Regular Users can register and sign in with a username and password, browse a catalog of internal “items” (such as songs, features, or content bundles), and contribute reviews with a 1–5 star rating plus optional text and up to 6 images per review; the Vue.js interface provides immediate validation feedback, autosaves draft text every 10 seconds locally in the browser, and prevents accidental double-posting with a visible “Submitting…” lock for 3 seconds. Users can also ask and answer Q&A entries on an item and maintain favorites/wishlists that sync across their devices once connected to the local server. Moderators work from a unified queue to review reported content, apply sensitive-word rules, and process appeals with clear outcomes (approved, rejected, or needs edit) and required moderator notes. Product Analysts use an interactive dashboard to filter by item, time window, sentiment label, and keywords, then explore linked charts (trends, heatmaps, word clouds, topic distributions, and co-occurrence relationships) with drill-down from aggregate to individual review sessions; custom views can be saved and shared inside the system as read-only links that expire after 7 days. Analysts can also run A/B tests for UI or wording changes, using an offline canary rollout and on-screen confidence indicators to decide whether to keep or roll back a variant. The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend, with MySQL as the system of record for users, roles (Administrator, Moderator, Product Analyst, Regular User), reviews, images, reports, appeals, Q&A, favorites, experiments, and event logs. Critical operations (posting a review, filing a report, recording an experiment exposure) must be idempotent for 10 minutes using server-side request tokens, and database writes use transactions to keep rating aggregates consistent; rating summaries refresh within 60 seconds and exclude suspected fraud until cleared. Behavior tracking records impressions, clicks, dwell time (in 1-second buckets, capped at 10 minutes per session), favorites, shares, and comments with session attribution, deduplication (same event within 2 seconds collapses), and anti-fraud rules that flag accounts exceeding 300 events per hour or repeating identical sequences across sessions; preference scoring uses configurable per-event weights editable by Product Analysts without redeploy. Security includes salted password hashing (Argon2id), local-only CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins in 15 minutes, IP allowlist/denylist support, CSRF/XSS/SQL injection protections, HTTPS for all traffic within the local network, and permission audit logs with masked sensitive fields. File upload is restricted to JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 5 MB per image, with format sniffing and hash-based duplicate detection; suspicious files are quarantined for moderator review rather than scanned via any external service. Reliability covers structured exception/performance monitoring dashboards, API rate limiting (60 requests/minute per user), scheduled jobs for nightly backups at 2:00 AM with weekly recovery drills, and scheduled report generation/data sync between tables to keep analytics views fast and recoverable without any internet dependency.
